judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but deeply unprofitable with a highly leveraged balance sheet.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 16.3%; next earnings date 2026-05-07.
positives: Revenue growth 111.6% YoY — demonstrates strong market adoption.
Gross margin 69.4% — indicates efficient service delivery before operating expenses.
Mean analyst target $138.90 suggests significant upside potential.
negatives: Operating margin -6.9% and Profit margin -25.6% — significant losses on operations.
Forward P/E -161.53 and negative earnings growth estimates — priced for future turnaround.
Debt/Equity 738.54 and Total debt $35.15B vs Total cash $2.27B — extremely high leverage.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-8.56B — substantial cash burn.
Current ratio 0.32 and Quick ratio 0.25 — liquidity concerns.

news.paragraph: CoreWeave is reportedly building its enterprise business, particularly within financial services, and is being highlighted by hedge funds as a pick in AI supply chains. The company's significant compute costs for AI are also noted in recent film production budgets, underscoring its role in the burgeoning AI sector. These developments suggest growing demand and strategic positioning for CoreWeave, though its path to profitability remains a key focus.

headlines.title: AI supply chain, Gen Z spending among hedge fund picks at Sohn Hong Kong
This Cannes Film Cost $500,000 to Make. $400,000 Was AI Compute Costs.
CoreWeave Building Up Enterprise Business, Led By Financial Services
